example_crux: Sum Σe^{ikθ}=(e^{i(n+1)θ}−1)/(e^{iθ}−1) then take the real part.

name: Rational Parametrization of the Unit Circle
one_liner: Parametrizes points on x²+y²=1 by ((1−t²)/(1+t²),2t/(1+t²)) for rational sweeps.
trigger: rational points or Pythagorean triples
example_crux: Generate all Pythagorean triples via the rational parametrization of the circle.

name: Smoothing / Equal-Variable Method
one_liner: Adjusts variables toward equality while monotonically improving the objective to locate the extremum.
trigger: symmetric optimization where equality is suspected
example_crux: Show any deviation from equal angles decreases Σsin(A), forcing the max at A=B=C.

name: Cauchy–Schwarz Inequality
one_liner: Bounds a dot product or sum of products by the product of norms.
trigger: sum of products or a Σ(a²)·Σ(b²) structure
example_crux: Apply (Σa_ib_i)²≤(Σa_i²)(Σb_i²) to bound a weighted trig sum.

name: Power Mean Inequality
one_liner: Orders quadratic, arithmetic, geometric, and harmonic means of positive reals.
trigger: comparing different means of the same quantities
example_crux: Use QM≥AM on sinA,sinB,sinC to bound Σsin²A.

name: Abel Summation (Summation by Parts)
one_liner: Rewrites Σa_kb_k using partial sums to convert a hard sum into a telescoped/bounded one.
trigger: sum of products where one factor has nice partial sums
example_crux: Bound Σa_k·sin(kθ) using bounded partial sums of sin(kθ) via Abel summation.

name: Linear Recurrence / Characteristic Equation
one_liner: Solves constant-coefficient recurrences via roots of the characteristic polynomial.
trigger: sequence defined by a_n=c_1a_{n-1}+…
example_crux: Solve a_n=2cosθ·a_{n-1}−a_{n-2} to get a_n=cos(nθ)-type closed form.

name: Lagrange Interpolation
one_liner: Reconstructs a degree-n polynomial from its values at n+1 points.
trigger: polynomial known only at specific points
example_crux: Evaluate a degree-n polynomial at a new point from its values, via the interpolation formula.

name: Trigonometric Solution of the Cubic
one_liner: Solves the depressed cubic in the three-real-root case using cosine of a trisected angle.
trigger: cubic with three real roots (casus irreducibilis)
example_crux: Substitute x=2√(−p/3)cosθ to reduce t³+pt+q=0 to cos3θ=constant.

name: Majorization / Karamata
one_liner: Compares sums of a convex function over majorizing vs. majorized tuples.
trigger: convex function summed over ordered constrained tuples
example_crux: Since (A,B,C) is majorized by a more spread triple, Karamata bounds Σf(angle) for convex f.

name: Reflection Principle (Optimization)
one_liner: Reflects a point across a line to straighten a broken path and minimize total length.
trigger: minimize sum of distances via a line
example_crux: Reflect one endpoint over the mirror line so the shortest reflected path is a straight segment.

name: Angle Addition Formulas
one_liner: Expands sin/cos/tan of a sum or difference of two angles.
trigger: a trig function of a composite angle
example_crux: Expand sin(A+B)=sinAcosB+cosAsinB to relate combined and base angles.

name: Conjugate Root Theorem
one_liner: Real-coefficient polynomials have complex roots in conjugate pairs (and irrational surd conjugates similarly).
trigger: a nonreal or surd root of a rational polynomial
example_crux: Given a+bi is a root of a real polynomial, deduce a−bi is too and form the quadratic factor.

name: Periodicity in Functional Equations
one_liner: Derives and exploits a period from a functional relation to reduce arguments.
trigger: relation like f(x+a)=f(x) forced by identities
example_crux: Show f(x+a)=−f(x) implies period 2a, then evaluate f at reduced arguments.

name: Stewart's Theorem
one_liner: Relates a cevian length to the triangle's sides and the segments it cuts.
trigger: cevian length with side segments
example_crux: Apply man+dad=bmb+cnc to compute a median or angle-bisector length.

name: Vectors and Dot Product
one_liner: Uses u·v=|u||v|cosθ to compute angles, projections, and prove perpendicularity.
trigger: angles between directions or projection lengths
example_crux: Show two segments are perpendicular by verifying their vector dot product is zero.

name: Extended Law of Sines / Circumradius
one_liner: Ties side/sine ratios to the circumradius via a/sinA=2R for circle-triangle problems.
trigger: connecting a triangle to its circumcircle
example_crux: Use 2R=a/sinA to express an inscribed chord in terms of its subtended angle.

name: Ravi Substitution
one_liner: Substitutes a=y+z, b=z+x, c=x+y with x,y,z>0 to encode the triangle inequality automatically.
trigger: inequality over sides of a triangle
example_crux: Replace triangle sides by Ravi variables so the constraint vanishes and AM-GM applies.

name: Half-Angle Formulas
one_liner: Expresses sin(θ/2), cos(θ/2), tan(θ/2) via cosθ, often removing square roots.
trigger: half of a known angle or reducing power
example_crux: Use tan(θ/2)=(1−cosθ)/sinθ to simplify a half-angle expression exactly.

name: Triangle Angle Identities
one_liner: Exploits A+B+C=π to derive relations like tanA+tanB+tanC=tanA·tanB·tanC.
trigger: symmetric trig relation among triangle angles
example_crux: From A+B+C=π deduce tanA+tanB+tanC=tanA·tanB·tanC to close an identity.

name: AM-GM Inequality
one_liner: Bounds an arithmetic mean below by the geometric mean of nonnegative reals.
trigger: you see a sum you want to compare to a product or vice versa
example_crux: Splitting a term into equal pieces so AM-GM gives the target product with equality at the desired point.

name: Weighted AM-GM
one_liner: Generalizes AM-GM with prescribed weights summing to one.
trigger: equality case is not at all-equal variables
example_crux: Choosing weights so the forced equality point matches the conjectured extremum.

name: AM-HM Inequality
one_liner: Arithmetic mean is at least the harmonic mean.
trigger: sums of reciprocals appear
example_crux: Bounding a sum of reciprocals by n^2 over the sum of the terms.

name: Cauchy-Schwarz Inequality
one_liner: Bounds a dot product by the product of norms of two sequences.
trigger: products of paired terms or sums to be decoupled
example_crux: Pairing terms so the cross-sum squared is controlled by the product of two sums-of-squares.

name: Titu's Lemma (Engel Form)
one_liner: Sum of a_i^2/b_i is at least (sum a_i)^2 over sum b_i.
trigger: sum of squares-over-positives fractions
example_crux: Rewriting each fraction as a square over its denominator to collapse to one clean fraction.

name: Hölder's Inequality
one_liner: Bounds a sum of products by a product of Lp norms with conjugate exponents.
trigger: three or more sequences, or need a cubed/power version of Cauchy
example_crux: Multiplying several copies of a sum so the product's cube dominates a cubed linear form.

name: Minkowski's Inequality
one_liner: Triangle inequality for Lp norms of sequences.
trigger: sums of square roots of sums of squares
example_crux: Treating terms as vector lengths so the total path exceeds the straight-line displacement.

name: Power Mean Inequality
one_liner: Means of higher order dominate means of lower order.
trigger: comparing quadratic, arithmetic, geometric, harmonic means
example_crux: Replacing a sum of squares by the square of the sum divided by n via QM-AM.

name: Jensen's Inequality
one_liner: For convex f, the mean of f exceeds f of the mean.
trigger: a single convex/concave function applied to each variable
example_crux: Recognizing the summand as a convex function so the extremum sits at equal arguments.

name: Karamata's Inequality (Majorization)
one_liner: If one tuple majorizes another, convex sums preserve the order.
trigger: sorted sequences with dominating partial sums
example_crux: Showing one ordered tuple majorizes another and applying a convex function termwise.

name: Muirhead's Inequality
one_liner: Compares symmetric sums via majorization of exponent tuples.
trigger: fully symmetric monomial sums with comparable exponents
example_crux: Reducing a symmetric polynomial inequality to a majorization check between exponent vectors.

name: Schur's Inequality
one_liner: Gives a nonnegative symmetric expression in nonnegative reals for t>0.
trigger: symmetric three-variable inequalities Muirhead can't close
example_crux: Adding the Schur term to bridge the gap left after expanding a symmetric inequality.

name: Rearrangement Inequality
one_liner: Similarly-ordered sequences maximize the sum of paired products.
trigger: sum of products of two sequences you may reorder
example_crux: Sorting both sequences the same way to maximize a pairing sum.

name: Chebyshev's Sum Inequality
one_liner: For like-sorted sequences, the mean of products exceeds the product of means.
trigger: two monotone-together sequences summed
example_crux: Factoring an ordered sum into n times the product of two averages.

name: Abel Summation
one_liner: Summation by parts converting a sum into partial-sum weighted differences.
trigger: sums with one monotone factor and controllable partial sums
example_crux: Grouping via partial sums so a monotone weight bounds the whole expression.

name: Bernoulli's Inequality
one_liner: (1+x)^r is at least 1+rx for suitable r and x>-1.
trigger: powers of expressions near 1
example_crux: Linearizing a power close to one to bound a product from below.

name: Young's Inequality
one_liner: ab is at most a^p/p + b^q/q for conjugate exponents.
trigger: splitting a product into additive powered pieces
example_crux: Bounding a stubborn cross-product by tunable powers to match other terms.

name: Maclaurin's Inequality
one_liner: Normalized elementary symmetric means form a decreasing chain.
trigger: chains of symmetric-function averages
example_crux: Ordering the symmetric averages p1 >= sqrt(p2) >= ... to interpolate a bound.

name: Newton's Inequalities
one_liner: Relates consecutive normalized elementary symmetric polynomials of real-rooted data.
trigger: log-concavity of symmetric functions of real numbers
example_crux: Using p_k^2 >= p_{k-1}p_{k+1} to constrain symmetric-function values.

name: SOS (Sum of Squares) Method
one_liner: Rewrites a difference as a sum of squares times nonnegative coefficients.
trigger: symmetric inequality after clearing denominators
example_crux: Expressing LHS-RHS as S_a(b-c)^2 + S_b(c-a)^2 + S_c(a-b)^2 with nonnegative S.

name: SOS-Schur Criterion
one_liner: Closes SOS forms with sign-varying coefficients using ordering criteria.
trigger: SOS coefficients not all nonnegative
example_crux: Assuming a>=b>=c and pairing squares so partial negativity is dominated.

name: Tangent Line Trick
one_liner: Bounds a convex summand below by its tangent at the equality point.
trigger: sum of single-variable convex terms with a fixed linear constraint
example_crux: Replacing each term by its tangent line at the equality value so the sum linearizes.

name: Smoothing / Adjustment
one_liner: Moves two variables toward each other to steer toward the extremum.
trigger: symmetric objective with a fixed constraint
example_crux: Showing any unequal pair can be equalized without worsening the objective.

name: Mixing Variables (MV)
one_liner: Reduces variables by replacing pairs with their mean or a boundary value.
trigger: symmetric multivariable inequality resisting direct attack
example_crux: Proving the minimum occurs when two variables coincide, then checking that case.

name: uvw / pqr Method
one_liner: Recasts symmetric three-variable inequalities in p,q,r (or u,v,w) and uses boundary behavior.
trigger: symmetric three-variable inequality, linear or convex in one symmetric variable
example_crux: Fixing p,q and pushing r to its extreme (two variables equal or one zero).

name: Ravi Substitution
one_liner: Replaces triangle sides with a=y+z, b=z+x, c=x+y for positive x,y,z.
trigger: triangle-side inequalities with triangle constraints
example_crux: Turning the triangle inequality constraint into positivity of new variables.

name: Normalization
one_liner: Fixes a scale (e.g. sum or product = 1) using homogeneity.
trigger: homogeneous inequality with free scaling
example_crux: Setting a+b+c=1 or abc=1 to eliminate a degree of freedom.

name: Homogenization
one_liner: Multiplies terms by powers of a constraint to equalize degrees.
trigger: constrained inequality with mismatched degrees
example_crux: Using a+b+c=1 to raise low-degree terms so Muirhead/SOS applies.

name: Lagrange Multipliers
one_liner: Locates constrained extrema via gradient proportionality.
trigger: smooth objective with smooth equality constraint
example_crux: Finding candidate extrema then arguing the global bound from them and the boundary.

name: Trigonometric Substitution
one_liner: Introduces angles to exploit identities and bounded ranges.
trigger: constraints like a^2+b^2=1 or abc+... resembling tangent identities
example_crux: Substituting a=tan(A/2) so a constraint becomes a triangle-angle relation.

name: Convexity / Concavity Argument
one_liner: Uses second-derivative sign to place extrema at endpoints or centers.
trigger: objective monotone-convex in each variable separately
example_crux: Since the function is convex per variable, the extremum lies on the constraint boundary.

name: Buffalo Way
one_liner: Brute substitution a=b+... with ordered variables reducing to a nonnegative polynomial.
trigger: symmetric inequality where all else fails and grinding is acceptable
example_crux: Setting c=min and a=c+s, b=c+t to reduce to visibly nonnegative polynomial in s,t,c.

name: Nesbitt's Inequality
one_liner: Sum of a/(b+c) over three positives is at least 3/2.
trigger: cyclic fractions with pairwise-sum denominators
example_crux: Adding 1 to each fraction to reveal a Cauchy/AM-HM structure.

name: Popoviciu's Inequality
one_liner: A three-point convexity refinement mixing pairwise and central averages.
trigger: convex function on three points with midpoint terms
example_crux: Applying the convex three-variable identity to close a symmetric sum.

name: RCF/LCF Theorems (Cirtoaje)
one_liner: Right/left convex function theorems handle sums where variables cluster at one side.
trigger: symmetric sum of a function convex only on part of the domain
example_crux: Reducing to the case n-1 variables equal via one-sided convexity.

name: Equal Variable Method (EV)
one_liner: Extrema of symmetric functions under fixed power-sums force most variables equal.
trigger: fixed sum and sum-of-squares (or power sums) constraints
example_crux: Showing the extremum has all-but-one variables equal, then checking that configuration.

name: WLOG Ordering
one_liner: Assumes a sorting of variables using symmetry to simplify casework.
trigger: symmetric or cyclic inequality with orderable variables
example_crux: Assuming a>=b>=c so difference signs become fixed for SOS or rearrangement.

name: Triangle Inequality
one_liner: Norm of a sum is at most the sum of norms.
trigger: absolute values, distances, or vector sums
example_crux: Bounding a sum of distances by routing through auxiliary points.

name: Discriminant Method
one_liner: Forces a quadratic to be nonnegative by controlling its discriminant.
trigger: inequality reducible to a quadratic in one variable
example_crux: Treating the expression as a quadratic and requiring nonpositive discriminant.

name: Probabilistic / Expectation Method
one_liner: Interprets a sum as an expectation and uses probabilistic inequalities.
trigger: average-of-random-choice interpretation available
example_crux: Realizing the inequality as Jensen or convexity applied to a random variable.

name: Integral Representation
one_liner: Rewrites a term as an integral to apply monotonicity or Cauchy-Schwarz for integrals.
trigger: sums or terms expressible as integrals of positive functions
example_crux: Writing 1/k as an integral so a sum becomes an integral amenable to bounding.

name: Telescoping Bound
one_liner: Bounds each term by a difference so the sum collapses.
trigger: sum you want to compare to a closed form
example_crux: Finding f with term <= f(k)-f(k+1) so the whole sum bounds by f(1).

name: Extremal Principle
one_liner: Considers the configuration achieving the max/min to derive constraints.
trigger: prove a bound is attained or study the tightest case
example_crux: Assuming a maximizing configuration and showing any perturbation is impossible or improves it.

name: Assume-the-Extremum Contradiction
one_liner: Supposes the inequality fails maximally and derives an impossibility.
trigger: sharp inequality where equality structure is rigid
example_crux: Taking a minimal counterexample and shrinking it to violate minimality.

name: Clever Substitution
one_liner: Introduces new variables to simplify structure or constraints.
trigger: symmetric ratios, or constraints like abc=1
example_crux: Setting x=a/b, y=b/c, z=c/a with xyz=1 to homogenize a cyclic inequality.

name: Logarithmic Convexity
one_liner: Takes logs to convert products into convex sums for Jensen/Karamata.
trigger: products of powers with a sum constraint
example_crux: Applying Jensen to log to bound a product of variable powers.

name: Lagrange's Identity
one_liner: Expresses the Cauchy-Schwarz gap as an explicit sum of squared minors.
trigger: need the exact defect in Cauchy-Schwarz
example_crux: Writing (sum a^2)(sum b^2)-(sum ab)^2 as sum of (a_i b_j - a_j b_i)^2.

name: Cauchy Reverse / Isolated Fudging
one_liner: Estimates a fraction from below by subtracting a controllable AM-GM term.
trigger: denominators blocking a direct Cauchy application
example_crux: Writing a^2/(a+b) = a - ab/(a+b) then bounding the subtracted piece by AM-GM.

name: Ptolemy's Inequality
one_liner: For four points, product of diagonals is at most sum of products of opposite sides.
trigger: four points with distance products, equality on a circle
example_crux: Applying the distance-product inequality with equality forcing concyclicity.

name: Weitzenböck's Inequality
one_liner: Sum of squared triangle sides is at least 4√3 times the area.
trigger: relating side lengths to area of a triangle
example_crux: Applying the a^2+b^2+c^2 >= 4√3·Area bound, refined by Hadwiger-Finsler.

name: Erdős–Mordell Inequality
one_liner: Sum of distances to vertices is at least twice the sum of distances to sides.
trigger: interior point distances to sides and vertices
example_crux: Projecting the interior point and summing trigonometric distance bounds.

name: Induction on Number of Variables
one_liner: Reduces an n-variable inequality to fewer variables.
trigger: inequality naturally parameterized by n
example_crux: Merging two variables or peeling one off to invoke the (n-1)-case.

name: Tangent Plane / Multivariable Linearization
one_liner: Bounds a multivariable convex function by its supporting hyperplane at equality.
trigger: multivariable convex objective with linear constraint
example_crux: Replacing the objective by its tangent plane at the symmetric point to linearize.
